name: pynxtools-apm

metadata.name: pynxtools-apm
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Make atom probe tomography and field-ion microscopy results interoperable via NeXus

documentation:
[![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)
![](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm/actions/workflows/pytest.yml/badge.svg)
![](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm/actions/workflows/pylint.yml/badge.svg)
![](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm/actions/workflows/publish.yml/badge.svg)
![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pynxtools-apm)
![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pynxtools-apm)
![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pynxtools-apm)
![](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm/badge.svg?branch=main)

# Parse and normalize atom probe tomography and field-ion microscopy data

## Installation
It is recommended to use python 3.11 with a dedicated virtual environment for this package.
Learn how to manage [python versions](https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv) and
[virtual environments](https://realpython.com/python-virtual-environments-a-primer/).

This package is a reader plugin for [`pynxtools`](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools) and thus should be installed together with `pynxtools`:
```shell
pip install pynxtools[apm]
```

for the latest release version from [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/pynxtools-em/).

If you are interested in the newest version, we recommend to work with a development installation instead.

## Purpose
This reader plugin for [`pynxtools`](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools) is used to translate diverse file formats from the scientific community and technology partners
within the field of atom probe tomography and field-ion microscopy into a standardized representation using the
[NeXus](https://www.nexusformat.org/) application definition [NXapm](https://fairmat-nfdi.github.io/nexus_definitions/classes/contributed_definitions/NXapm.html#nxapm).

## Supported file formats
This plugin supports the majority of the file formats that are currently used for atom probe.
A detailed summary is available in the [reference section of the documentation](https://fairmat-nfdi.github.io/pynxtools-apm).

## Getting started
[A getting started tutorial](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm/tree/main/examples) is offered that guides you
on how to use the apm reader for converting your data to NeXus using a Jupyter notebook or command line calls. Note that not every combination of input from a supported file format and other input, such as from an electronic lab notebook, allows filling the required and recommended fields and their attributes of the NXapm application definition. Therefore, you may need to provide an ELN file that contains the missing values in order for the validation step of the APM reader to pass.

## Contributing
We are continously working on improving the collection of parsers and their functionalities.
If you would like to implement a parser for your data, feel free to get in contact.

## Development install
Install the package with its dependencies:

```shell
git clone https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools-apm.git --branch main --recursive pynxtools_apm
cd pynxtools_apm
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -e ".[dev,docs]"
pre-commit install
```

The last line installs a [pre-commit hook](https://pre-commit.com/#intro) which
automatically formats (linting) and type checks the code before committing.

## Test this software
Especially relevant for developers, there exists a basic test framework written in
[pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/) which can be used as follows:

```shell
python -m pytest -sv tests
```

## Contact person in FAIRmat for this reader
[Markus Kühbach](https://www.fairmat-nfdi.eu/fairmat/about-fairmat/team-fairmat)

classes.name: pynxtools_apm.reader.APMReader

methods.name: pynxtools_apm.reader.APMReader.read
documentation: Read data from given file, return filled template dictionary apm.

Parse content from community file formats.

Specifically, (local electrode) atom probe microscopy and field-ion microscopy
into a NXapm.nxdl-compliant NeXus file.
from pynxtools_apm.utils.apm_generate_synthetic_data import (
ApmCreateExampleData,
this apm parser combines multiple sub-parsers
so we need the following input:
> logical analysis which use case
> data input from an ELN (currently NOMAD Oasis, eLabFTW, openBIS in the future)
> data input from technology partner files
> functionalities for creating default plots
> developer functionalities for creating synthetic data
for development purposes synthetic datasets can be created which that are for now stored
all in the same file. As these use the same dictionary, the template variable analyses
of files which are larger than the physical main memory is currently not supported

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.concepts.mapping_functors_pint.var_path_to_spcfc_path
documentation: Transforms a variadic path to an actual path with instances.
general conversion workflow
1. Normalize src data to str, bool, or ureg.Quantity
These ureg.Quantities should use numpy scalar or array for the dtype of the magnitude.
Use special NeXus unit categories unitless, dimensionless, and any.
2. Map on specific trg path, ureg.Unit, eventually with conversions, and dtype conversion
Later this could include endianness
3. Store ureg.Quantity magnitude and if non-special also correctly converted @units
attribute

methods.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.oasis_eln_reader.NxApmNomadOasisElnSchemaParser.parse
documentation: Copy data from self into template the appdef instance.

Parse eln_data.yaml dump file content generated from a NOMAD Oasis YAML.

This parser implements a design where an instance of a specific NOMAD
custom schema ELN template is used to fill pieces of information which
are typically not contained in files from technology partners
(e.g. pos, epos, apt, rng, rrng, ...). Until now, this custom schema and
the NXapm application definition do not use a fully harmonized vocabulary.
Therefore, the here hardcoded implementation is needed which maps specifically
named pieces of information from the custom schema instance on named fields
in an instance of NXapm

The functionalities in this ELN YAML parser do not check if the
instantiated template yields an instance which is compliant with NXapm.
Instead, this task is handled by the generic part of the dataconverter
during the verification of the template dictionary.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.load_ranging.extract_data_from_env_file
documentation: Add those required information which a ENV file has.
modify the template to take into account ranging
ranging is currently not resolved recursively because
ranging(NXprocess) is a group which has a minOccurs=1, \er
maxOccurs="unbounded" set of possible named
NXion members, same case for more than one operator
ion indices are on the interval [0, 256)

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_case_one
documentation: Handle specification of matrix or remainder element.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_case_two
documentation: Handle case element and at.-% composition, no comp. stdev.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_case_three
documentation: Handle case element with different than default normalization, no comp. stdev.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_case_four
documentation: Handle case at.-% normalization with comp. stdev.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_case_five
documentation: Handle case with different than standard normalization and comp. stdev.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_human_readable_composition_information
documentation: Identify instruction to parse from eln_input to define composition table.

functions.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.parse_composition_table.parse_composition_table
documentation: Check if all the entries in the composition list yield a valid composition table.

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.RECON_HEIGHT
documentation: angstroem

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.RECON_RADIUS
documentation: angstroem

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.MAX_COMPONENTS
documentation: how many different molecular ions in one dataset/entry

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.MAX_ATOMS
documentation: determine power-law fraction of n_atoms per ion

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.MULTIPLES_FACTOR
documentation: controls how likely multiple ions are synthesized

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.MAX_CHARGE_STATE
documentation: highest allowed charge_state of an ion

variables.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.MAX_ATOMIC_NUMBER
documentation: do not include heavier atoms than Plutonium

classes.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.ApmCreateExampleData

methods.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.ApmCreateExampleData.create_reconstructed_positions
documentation:
Create aggregate of specifically oriented

crystallographic unit cells and take the positions
in this lattice to carve out a portion of the
point cloud representing the reconstruction positions.

methods.name: pynxtools_apm.utils.generate_synthetic_data.ApmCreateExampleData.place_atoms_from_periodic_table
documentation:
Sample elements from the periodic table

create (hypothetical) charged molecular ions from them
and evaluate their mass-to-charge-state ratio to be used
as values in the example dataset.
